facts.primaryTitle: The Truth Behind the Russian Sleep Experiment
facts.setting: The experiment promised freedom if five prisoners stayed awake for thirty days.
criticalEvents: The experiment promised freedom if five prisoners stayed awake for thirty days.
On day nine, they stopped asking to be released.
Anya Volkov was a physician assigned to observe five prisoners when the story began in a fictional Cold War research bunker.
facts.threat: The experiment promised freedom if five prisoners stayed awake for thirty days.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Anya Volkov defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
